# ConeZ scanlist v1 -- channels a cone scans to find the master's beacon.
#
# One entry per line. The first token selects the mode (L=LoRa, F=FSK), optionally
# followed by flag letters: X = receive-only (listen, never transmit here),
# W = FSK data whitening (F entries only; seed 0x01FF). Frequencies in Hz, sync hex:
#   L[X]    <freq_hz> <bw_hz> <sf> <cr> <sync_hex>                   LoRa
#   F[X][W] <freq_hz> <bitrate_bps> <freqdev_hz> <rxbw_hz> <sync_hex>  FSK
#
# '#' comments and blank lines are ignored. Scan order = file order. The cone
# dwells on each entry longer than the beacon period before advancing, and
# locks to the first entry where it hears a master beacon.

# Default channel first (most likely):
L 431250000 500000 7 5 0xDEAD

# LoRa fallbacks (other BW/SF on the 70 cm amateur band):
L 431250000 125000 9 5 0x12
L 433000000 500000 7 5 0xDEAD
L 432000000 250000 9 5 0x12

# FSK (Phase 9): GFSK 4800 bps, 9.6 kHz deviation (h=4.0), 29.3 kHz DSB RX bandwidth.
# The 29.3 kHz RX BW is what makes the link lossless (19.5 kHz lost ~4.6% even at
# 4800 Hz dev); the wider deviation is only a secondary offset-margin factor.
# sync word DEAD (matches LoRa). Emitted by the master when [lora] mode=fsk. Sync is bare hex (no
# 0x) for parse_hex_syncword(). The W flag (FW) enables data whitening (default on,
# seed 0x01FF); drop it (plain F) for NRZ.
FW 431250000 4800 9600 29300 DEAD
